elements.title: Marie Salmon
elements.contents: ... that Marie Salmon (pictured) was condemned to be burned at the stake for a crime she didn't commit, but narrowly avoided execution by claiming she was pregnant?

elements.title: articulated tourist buses
elements.contents: ... that Yokohama's articulated tourist buses feature a matte metallic blue livery inspired by the city?

elements.title: Osyp Turiansky
elements.contents: ... that Ukrainian author Osyp Turiansky's works were suppressed in his homeland?

elements.title: Until the End of Time
elements.contents: ... that a reviewer called "Until the End of Time" "excessively bland"?

elements.title: Taher al-Aqili
elements.contents: ... that the current defense minister of Yemen Taher al-Aqili was injured in a landmine explosion while serving as chief of staff?

elements.title: in 1972 chose
elements.contents: ... that Montana voters in 1972 chose to keep the death penalty, allow for gambling to be legalized, have a bicameral legislature, and ratify a new constitution?

elements.title: John Humble
elements.contents: ... that photographer John Humble used harsh late afternoon light to flatten the Los Angeles landscape?

elements.title: Curitiba Pride
elements.contents: ... that there have been two unsuccessful proposals to make Curitiba Pride intangible cultural heritage?

elements.title: a tax avoidance scheme
elements.contents: ... that "cannibalism, group sex, and snail orgies" hindered a tax avoidance scheme by the son of Tommy Ball?
